title: Airbus, Indonesia Partner to Produce Bio-Based Sustainable Aviation Fuel
content: Airbusâ€™s Singapore branch is teaming up with researchers from Indonesiaâ€™s Bogor Agricultural University (IPB) in West Java to develop sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) from biomass. The project aims to produce up to 100 million tonnes of SAF annually using organic feedstocks such as crop residues, wood, and plant waste. The worldâ€™s largest crude palm oil producer, Indonesia has also been testing the conversion of CPO and waste cooking oil into bioavtur since 2021, with the first trial flight planned for August if the fuel meets standards.
author: Analysts Account
